title: eriogonum campanulatum subsp. orendense
intro: Eriogonum campanulatum subsp. orendense, also known as Oregon buckwheat or Orenda buckwheat, is a species of flowering plant in the buckwheat family. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Eriogonum campanulatum subsp. orendense include its potential to soothe skin irritations, reduce fever, and support immune function, making it a popular choice in herbal remedies. Herbal preparations of the plant often involve drying and infusing the leaves and stems in hot water or oil to create teas, tinctures, and salves.
